title: Team Norms: Incident Response
bodyMarkdown:
How the Helios team runs an incident, from first page to writeup.

## Declaring an incident
Any customer-facing degradation gets a JIRA incident opened immediately, even before root cause is known. Do not wait for confirmation that something is actually broken, false alarms are cheap, silent outages are not.

## During the incident
One person drives, everyone else stays out of their way unless asked. The driver posts status updates at a steady cadence, even a no-change update, so people are not left guessing. Mitigation comes before root cause: roll back a bad deploy or fail over a bad host before spending time understanding why it broke.

## Resolution
An incident is resolved once customer impact has stopped, not once root cause is fully understood. Root cause work can continue afterward and often turns into a follow-up ticket or an RFC.

## Writeup
Every incident gets a comment summarizing root cause and fix once known, so future on-calls searching the history find it. Several of our runbooks exist because an incident writeup got promoted into one.

## Owner
Elena Petrova owns this process, propose changes to her directly.
